fields.description: YARN-5326 proposes adding native support for recurring reservations in the YARN ReservationSystem. This JIRA is a sub-task to track the changes required in ReservationAgent to accomplish it. Please refer to the design doc in the parent JIRA for details.

Thanks [~curino] for working on this. The patch looks straightforward.

Couple of nits:
* In {{PlanningAlgorithm}}, check for null before parsing the _recurrenceExpression_.
* Again, I really like the {{TestReservationAgents}} but why are we not initializing the parameters in respective tests as is done in {{BaseSharingPolicyTest}}?
* Fix the checkstyle (naming) and whitespace Yetus warnings.

Thanks @subru for the feedback. 

# Regarding {{recurrenceExpression}} in {{PlanningAlgorithm}} it is never null by design (see {{ReservationDefinitionPBImpl.getRecurrenceExpression()}} is set to "0" if there is no recurrence), so I think the null check is redundant (unless we assume other non-PBImpl implementations will be used in the future).
# This is not really a base tests, as we already had substantial tests in the specific per-agents tests, which checks for specific corner cases. Migrating those would be a delicate/painful, so this test was added, to provide a broader "lots of noisy allocations" type experiments, which in my experience are good to exercise a larger portion of the codebase and capture hard-to-spot multi-reservation scenarios.
# done

Tests pass locally when YARN-5330 is applied (but lets see what YETUS says when that one is in)

Thanks [~curino] for working on this. The patch looks straightforward.

Couple of nits:
* In {{PlanningAlgorithm}}, check for null before parsing the _recurrenceExpression_.
* Again, I really like the {{TestReservationAgents}} but why are we not initializing the parameters in respective tests as is done in {{BaseSharingPolicyTest}}?
* Fix the checkstyle (naming) and whitespace Yetus warnings.

Thanks @subru for the feedback. 

# Regarding {{recurrenceExpression}} in {{PlanningAlgorithm}} it is never null by design (see {{ReservationDefinitionPBImpl.getRecurrenceExpression()}} is set to "0" if there is no recurrence), so I think the null check is redundant (unless we assume other non-PBImpl implementations will be used in the future).
# This is not really a base tests, as we already had substantial tests in the specific per-agents tests, which checks for specific corner cases. Migrating those would be a delicate/painful, so this test was added, to provide a broader "lots of noisy allocations" type experiments, which in my experience are good to exercise a larger portion of the codebase and capture hard-to-spot multi-reservation scenarios.
# done

Tests pass locally when YARN-5330 is applied (but lets see what YETUS says when that one is in)
